name: First New York
description_short: First New York Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit credit union serving Albany, Schenectady, and surrounding NY counties since 1937, offering checking, savings, loans, and investment services.
description_long:
First New York Federal Credit Union was founded in 1937 as Schenectady Teachers Federal Credit Union and has evolved into a regional financial institution serving members across Albany, Schenectady, Rensselaer, Saratoga, and Schoharie counties in New York. As a not-for-profit credit union, the organization's stated mission centers on serving member financial needs rather than generating shareholder profits.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of banking and lending products including auto loans (as low as 3.99% APR), mortgages, home equity lines of credit (as low as 1.99% APR), personal loans, share certificates (9-month CDs at 4.00%), Visa credit cards (promotional 4.99% APR), and solar-specific financing. They also provide investment services and maintain a KIDS Banking program for minors requiring only $2 to open an account. Members can access their accounts through online banking, mobile platforms, and multiple physical branch locations across their service area.

The credit union distinguishes itself through member recognition—it has been voted "Best Credit Union" by members for three consecutive years (as of the website's 2026 content). The organization emphasizes relationship-based banking with features like Financial Wellness Checks, personalized loan consultations, and same-day to next-day processing on certain products. Member testimonials highlight exceptional customer service, competitive rates compared to traditional banks, and fast loan approval processes. They also maintain active fraud education through their "Safety First" initiative.

As a regional credit union with membership eligibility requirements (likely tied to geographic location or affiliation with founding institutions), First New York may not be accessible to all consumers nationwide. While the website showcases strong member satisfaction and competitive rates, their service area is geographically limited to a specific region of upstate New York, making them unsuitable for consumers outside this territory. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Visa credit cards with promotional APR options
Financial Wellness Checks and advisory services
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 3.99% APR for new and used vehicles
Low home equity loan rates as low as 1.99% APR with tax advantages
Voted "Best Credit Union" three years running by members based on service quality
Fast loan processing with reported 24-hour approval timelines in member reviews
Share certificates (CDs) offering 4.00% APR for 9-month terms
Not-for-profit structure means profits return to members through better rates and lower fees
Specialized solar loan program that consolidates solar installation costs into single financing
cons: Geographic service area limited to five counties in upstate New York (Albany, Schenectady, Rensselaer, Saratoga, Schoharie), excluding the majority of U.S. consumers
Membership eligibility likely restricted—no clear explanation of who can join or potential employer/affiliation requirements
Limited transparency on full fee structures, account minimums, and overdraft policies on public-facing website
No evidence of mobile app or digital banking capabilities mentioned despite standard modern credit union offerings
Website does not disclose membership size, assets under management, or financial stability metrics
best_for: Residents and employers in Albany, Schenectady, Rensselaer, Saratoga, or Schoharie counties seeking competitive local lending
First-time homebuyers and refinancers in upstate New York looking for relationship-based mortgage assistance
Auto loan borrowers in the service area seeking rates below national bank averages (3.99%+)
Parents wanting to establish youth banking habits through the KIDS Banking program for minors
diagnosis: First New York is best for residents and businesses within its five-county upstate New York service area who prioritize relationship banking and competitive rates over convenience of nationwide branch access. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—membership eligibility appears restricted to the specific region served, making this option unavailable for consumers outside Albany, Schenectady, Rensselaer, Saratoga, and Schoharie counties.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
